turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Welcome! Thanks for coming in today. How are you doing? Can you start us off with a quick self-introduction?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'm doing well, thanks. I'm a product designer with about a decade of freelance and consulting experience across various industries.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Great. So, can you walk me through your most recent role or engagement?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: My most recent work was a six-month engagement with a fintech startup, running from late last year into early this year, focused on their onboarding redesign.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: And roughly how many years would you say you've been working with your top design skill?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'd estimate around eight years of focused product design work, though I've been designing in some form for closer to twelve.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: I noticed a specific project mentioned on your background — could you tell me more about that?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I wasn't sure which project you were referring to since none were named specifically, but I can highlight one of my favorites: a redesign of a healthcare patient portal.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What would you say was your biggest professional achievement at your most recent engagement?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: At that fintech startup, I led a redesign that improved their activation rate by roughly 35% within the first quarter after launch.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: That's solid. So why are you interested in this Senior Product Designer role with us?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'm looking to move from freelance work into something more long-term, and your company's design-first culture really stands out to me.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: You clearly have relevant experience. Could you speak to how your background aligns with what we're looking for?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I've spent years working directly with product and engineering teams to ship user-centered features, which I think maps well to your needs.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Now, I do see one potential gap — could you address that?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I assume you're referring to the lack of long-tenure roles on my resume. As a consultant, I've often embedded with teams for extended periods, so the work itself has been deeply collaborative despite the contract structure.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Got it. Let's talk about your education — where did you study, and what did you focus on?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I studied Visual Communication Design at [University], graduating about ten years ago with a focus on interaction design.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Can you walk me through a challenging situation using the STAR format?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Sure. Situation: a client needed a complete app redesign in eight weeks. Task: I had to lead research, design, and handoff solo. Action: I prioritized ruthlessly and ran tight weekly reviews. Result: we shipped on time and the client's NPS jumped 20 points.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What are your salary expectations for this role?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Based on my research and experience, I'd be looking somewhere in the range of $140,000 to $160,000, but I'm flexible depending on the full package.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Do you have any questions for us?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes — what does the design team's structure look like, and how does product design collaborate with engineering day-to-day?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Going back to that fintech engagement you mentioned — how long did that take, and what was the team size?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: It ran about six months, and the core team was four people including myself.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Quick one — do you have a valid driver's license?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes, I do — current and unrestricted.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: For what it's worth, I really enjoyed our chat today, and I'm looking forward to the next steps.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Before we wrap — are you open to relocation if needed, and would you be able to start within two weeks?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'm open to relocation for the right opportunity, and yes, two weeks' notice should be doable.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Great. So, how soon would you need an answer if we extended an offer, and are there other roles you're actively considering?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'd want at least a week to consider any offer, and yes, I'm in late stages with one other company, so timing does matter for me.
